hypothesis: Removed non-trivial MTRand() ctor from MTRand.hpp (getInstance was sole caller); MTRand is now trivially default-constructible so `static MTRand instance;` needs no compiler guard. Manually guard one-time init with `extern "C" s8 lbl_eu_80665580` (signed char, not u8/unsigned) + `if(!lbl_eu_80665580){ instance.srand(0x012BD6AA); instance.pNext = instance.state; lbl_eu_80665580 = 1; }`. s8 (not u8) is required to reproduce retail's `extsb.`+`bne` guard test (u8 compiles to `cmpwi r0,0`, a 1-insn diff, 99.3%). Result: 142/142 instructions byte-identical AND both R_PPC_EMB_SDA21 relocs at 0x8/0x150 now target lbl_eu_80665580 matching retail exactly (was @GUARD@getInstance__Q22ml6MTRandFv@instance). srand__Q22ml6MTRandFUl and nextMt__Q22ml6MTRandFv remain FULL_MATCH (100%); no other MTRand callers construct the class directly (only getInstance() is used, verified via grep).
next_change: Only remaining diff is @LOCAL@getInstance__Q22ml6MTRandFv@instance (base, WEAK-defined in our .bss) vs @LOCAL@getInstance__Q22ml6MTRandFv@instance_806561E0 (target, UND external) for the 2512-byte singleton storage. config/us/splits.txt gives MTRand.cpp a .text-only range (no .bss range), so decomp-toolkit does not attribute this .bss address to this split and must reference it externally with a decomp-toolkit-assigned address suffix in the retail split object; our compiled TU legitimately defines the storage locally (WEAK, same name as symbols.txt with no suffix) since it has nowhere else to put it. This suffix is a decomp-toolkit split/objdiff artifact, not derivable from MWCC-mangled names via any C++ source change under high-level-only policy -- likely a hard cap short of FULL_MATCH pending toolkit-side symbol aliasing.

hypothesis: Direct extern "C" const float lbl_eu_80667EB0 in both deadzone comparisons (replacing CFPAD_STICK_DEADZONE) reaches FULL_MATCH: 0 byte diff across all 868 bytes/217 insns, both @sda21 relocs now name-match retail. Prior 98.1% regression claim did not reproduce on this build (obj/objdiff-cli/mwcc versions in this checkout); MWCC kept identical fabs/lfs/frsp/fcmpo schedule when the extern is declared as plain top-level extern "C" const float (not touched via helper/inline) and used verbatim in both compares.

hypothesis: Implemented as high-level C++: CSplitFrame (unk45C) lazy-teardown when inactive, updateMsg() call, then two independent has-view1/has-view2 goto-chain gates over getView1/getView2 on the split frame -- first gating apply(), second (inside `if (mChildren.empty() && unk238.empty())`) gating wkSetEvent(EVT_NONE). Reused a single function-scope hasView1/hasView2 pair for both gates capped the match at 99.9% (one swapped li r30/r31 pair) because retail zero-inits the pair in opposite order in each gate and MWCC cannot honor both orders from one variable pair. Fix: block-scope a second, independent hasView1a/hasView2a pair for the first gate only, each pair's zero-assignment order tuned separately (first: hasView2a=0;hasView1a=0; second: hasView2=0;hasView1=0;) -> 0 byte diff across all 0x14C bytes.
next_change: None - target closed at FULL_MATCH. See docs/MWCC_REFERENCE.md #8b for the reusable per-block-scoped-temporaries pattern.
